title: Quality Gate Threshold Tuning
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: quality gate threshold tuning.
assumptions: Actions model distinct API operations or orchestration steps and are executed atomically from the governance controller.
Quality indicators, analyzers, scanners and threshold profiles are versioned and can be bound/unbound without data loss.
Human approvers or automated approver identities are available when workflows require manual approval steps.
Runner pools and credential artifacts are finite resources; allocation and release are required to avoid exhaustion.
risks: Mis-tuned thresholds (too strict) may cause excessive pipeline blockage and delivery delays.
Overly permissive thresholds may undermine security and quality objectives.
False positives from analyzers or scanners can trigger unnecessary remediation cycles and approvals.
Approval bottlenecks or unavailable approvers can stall policy-driven promotions.
Resource leaks (runners or credentials not returned) may reduce capacity for parallel evaluations.
